hero.title: Return to Nature, Peacefully
hero.subtitle: Contemporary biodegradable memorial urns designed with reverence, minimalism, and care for our planet.

philosophy.title: A Gentle Farewell
philosophy.text1: Kamibi Store reimagines the memorial process, turning traditional funerary items into beautiful, ecological, and deeply meaningful tributes. We believe that returning to nature is a final act of peace and profound love.
philosophy.text2: Crafted from 100% natural, organic and compostable materials, our urns are designed to return ashes harmoniously to water or earth, leaving only memories and new life behind.

water.title: Water Ceremony
water.desc: Specially aligned for ocean or sea scattering. Dissolves gracefully, returning the elements to the source of life.

earth.title: Earth Burial
earth.desc: Perfect for forest, garden, or natural cemetery burials. Integrates with the soil to foster new natural growth.

t1.quote: The Terra Urn ceremony in the forest gave us a sense of closure and connection we didn't think possible. Truly beautiful.
t1.author: Sarah M.

t2.quote: Seeing the Aqua Urn dissolve gently into the sea was peaceful and serene. It felt exactly like what she would have wanted.
t2.author: David K.

ceremonies.title: Ceremonies of Release
ceremonies.subtitle: Discover how Kamibi urns adapt beautifully to different rituals, offering a dignified return to nature.
ceremonies.waterDesc: A serene farewell designed for oceans, rivers, or lakes. The urn floats briefly, giving families a final peaceful moment before descending gracefully to dissolve.
ceremonies.waterStep1: Place ashes inside the water-soluble bag.
ceremonies.waterStep2: Seal with the biodegradable adhesive strip.
ceremonies.waterStep3: Lower gently into water. Complete dissolution occurs in 10-15 minutes, leaving zero residue.
ceremonies.earthDesc: A grounded tribute designed to integrate with the earth. Over a few weeks, the urn decomposes, returning nutrients to the soil and fostering new plant growth.
ceremonies.earthStep1: Place ashes in the organic compostable pouch.
ceremonies.earthStep2: Secure the lid using the natural clay seal.
ceremonies.earthStep3: Bury in soil. Wildflower seeds inside will sprout, creating a living memory.

timeline.title: The Lifecycle of Tribute
timeline.subtitle: A step-by-step journey of how our premium biodegradable urns return to nature without leaving a footprint.
timeline.step1Desc: Made of lightweight cellulose and plant fibers, all Kamibi urns are TSA-compliant for airport security carry-on, allowing stress-free travel to your ceremony location.
timeline.step2Desc: Whether placed on water or buried in a quiet forest, the urn provides a dignified, elegant focal point for family remembrance.
timeline.step3Desc: Without plastic linings, glues, or metals, the urn breaks down organically. Made with soy-based inks for a toxin-free biodegradation.
timeline.step4Desc: As it decomposes, the urn nourishes the earth and generates new life, transforming the site into a living tribute for future generations.

cta.title: Begin a Conscious Journey
cta.subtitle: Discover our collection of premium biodegradable urns and sharing sets.

store.title: Our Memorial Collection
store.subtitle: Choose an elegant, ecological pathway tailored to their memory.

step1.title: Remove the Cover
step1.desc: Carefully slide off the outer sleeve and prepare the urn for the ceremony.

step2.title: Prepare the Memories
step2.desc: Remove the included cards and pen to write your farewell messages.

step3.title: Place the Ashes
step3.hook: An act of love and respect
step3.desc: Carefully place the ashes inside the biodegradable urn.

step4.title: Write Your Message
step4.desc: Share memories, gratitude, or your last words for your loved one.

step5.title: Close the Urn
step5.desc: Secure the urn and place the sleeve back for an elegant finish.

step6.title: Transport with Care
step6.desc: Carry the urn peacefully to the chosen ceremony location.

step7.title: Prepare for Immersion
step7.hook: Ease its return to the sea
step7.desc: Follow the indicated preparation for a natural and harmonious immersion.

step8.title: Farewell with Serenity
step8.desc: Gently place the urn on the water and take a moment to honor their memory as it returns to nature.

benefits.title: Respectful to the Earth
benefits.b1_desc: Made of organic starch, natural fibers, and vegetable-based dyes.
benefits.b2_desc: Approved for carry-on air travel, letting you transport them safely.
benefits.b3_desc: Every urn comes with a biodegradable bag, twine, and memorial seed-card.
benefits.b4_desc: Guaranteed chemical-free, metal-free, and plastic-free materials.

table.f4_t: Highly marked up by funeral homes

complements.title: Sharing Memorial Sets
complements.desc: Share a small, symbolic portion of the ashes with close family members across generations.

hero.title:
Born so that
goodbye feels natural

manifesto.text: We believe death is not an ending. It is a return. And that return deserves to be as beautiful, as careful, and as full of life as everything that came before.

journey.title: Connecting past, present, and future
journey.t1Text: Kamibi was born from a simple question: why should a farewell tribute leave a footprint of plastic or metal that takes centuries to degrade? We began the search for a clean, dignified, and natural alternative.
journey.t2Text: We collaborated with artisans and botany specialists to formulate recycled cellulose fibers and water-soluble bags and threads to contain the ashes.
journey.t3Text: We launched our first collections for water (Aqua) and earth (Terra), designed to biodegrade naturally and allow ashes to return to the environment without contaminating, promoting an eco-friendly farewell.
journey.t4Text: Today we accompany thousands of families worldwide in respectful ceremonies, expanding our vision toward a future where every farewell returns life to the planet.

cycle.title: The journey of tribute
cycle.c1Desc: Handcrafted from recycled cellulose fibers and pure natural clay.
cycle.c2Desc: An elegant, biodegradable memorial that acts as a focal point of reverence.
cycle.c3Desc: Integrates with the elements naturally: minutes in water, weeks in earth.
cycle.c4Desc: Wildflower seeds bloom, attracting local wildlife and purifying the soil.

founders.title: The soul behind Kamibi
founders.text1: We don't just make urns. We design transitions. Every piece that leaves our studio carries the promise that love doesn't end, but transforms into air, water, and fertile earth.
founders.text2: From selecting organic raw materials to our recyclable packaging, every decision is guided by reverence. We believe honoring those we love should not compromise the earth that future generations will inherit.

materials.title: Only what the earth recognizes
materials.m2Desc: Pure mineral seal and structure, no chemical additives.
materials.m3Desc: Each earth urn contains seeds that bloom as a living memorial in the soil.
materials.m4Desc: Organic glues and natural inks that dissolve without a trace.

testimonial.quote: “Death is not extinguishing the light; it is only putting out the lamp because the dawn has come.”
testimonial.author: Rabindranath Tagore

cta.title: Create an unforgettable tribute
cta.desc: Contemporary biodegradable urns for a dignified and serene return.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Are the urns allowed on airplanes?
faq.a1: Yes, all our cellulose and mineral clay urns are TSA-approved for carry-on luggage.
faq.q2: How long do they take to dissolve?
faq.a2: Water urns dissolve completely within 10 to 15 minutes. Earth urns compost in the soil within 3 to 5 weeks.
faq.a3: Yes, we ship worldwide using secure, eco-friendly packaging.

thankYou.message: Every Kamibi urn is an act of love toward nature and the ones we cherish. Thank you for choosing an eco-friendly farewell.
